title: Running 128 Coding Agents at Once
summary: Cursor's Sam Whitmore discusses with Baseten executives the scaling of coding agents to hundreds of instances, agent-to-agent communication and review mechanisms, and the shift from parallel task management to building integrated agent systems.
writeup: Cursor's Sam Whitmore joins Baseten executives to discuss scaling coding agents to hundreds of concurrent instances, with the title referencing 128 agents running at once. The conversation covers agent-to-agent communication and review mechanisms, where agents check one another's work. The central shift is from managing parallel tasks to building integrated agent systems, framed alongside concepts like agent orchestration and test-time compute.
